markdownRemark.html:
Use Cases

Applicable in scenarios that require "fuzzy search," "custom rendering of filter boxes," "quick switching between filter condition groups and saving filter conditions to the backend," and "quick configuration of table display columns."

By setting queryBar to filterBar, you can set the display mode of the filter bar to a dynamic filter bar.

Types of Configuration

Supports dynamically configuring filter bars with query conditions. It can be used purely on the frontend or combined with standard backend interfaces to save filter lists.

Category 1: Usage Without Interface

Configure query condition field information through DataSet.queryFields. Like other filter conditions, the interaction state of filter conditions can be controlled using Field.Props properties such as dynamicProps/computedProps. Click to view Demo.

As shown in the figure above, the upper part of the table, except for the buttons operation buttons and the combineSort combined sorting button, is configured with dynamic filter bars. It is mainly divided into:

  1. prefixes/suffixes — the rendering area for the prefixes and suffixes of the search bar
  2. fuzzyQuery — the fuzzy search box area
  3. queryFields — the query conditions area
  4. advancedSearchFields — the advanced filter area
  5. conditionList — the query condition group switch area.

Category 2: Use in combination with interfaces

In this scenario, users have multiple query condition groups, and different query condition default values (or displays) are configured in different groups, so that users can switch between multiple query condition values.

Key attributes

  1. searchCode: The unique encoding of the backend interface of the dynamic filter bar (when the backend saves the filter item, you need to configure the unique encoding of the backend interface of the dynamic filter bar to ensure data matching)

● Platform recommendation coding specifications

  • Module name. page. Personalization features
  • All caps and numbers must start with letters and numbers, and can include "-", "_", ".", "/"
  • tableFilterAdapter: Dynamic filter bar request adapter (supports global configuration form)
As shown in the figure above, when the query condition group is "default condition", the query condition is set to "test" and the "age" is "22". Even if the number of default query conditions exceeds 'queryFieldsLimit', all default query conditions will be displayed automatically.

The backend returns data to the 'preset' or 'user' group according to its 'isTenant: boolean' parameter for display, and the default query conditions of the query condition group are saved through 'conditionList'https://hzero-test.open.hand-china.com/mock/filterlist.

Explanation and effect display of common scenes

Fuzzy search only

Scenario description: If you do not want to configure detailed search conditions and only query data through fuzzy search, you can set 'fuzzyQueryOnly' to 'true'.

Standalone use

Scene Description: Addresses issues when used with other components, avoiding complex style settings.

The DynamicFilterBar extracted from Table requires manually configuring the query fields to be displayed through queryFields. Query conditions in the DataSet that are not configured will not be displayed.

The effect is shown in the figure: only the filter bar is used, not constrained by the table body, allowing more freedom to define the page structure below.

The code example is as follows:

import { Table, TextField, NumberField, Select, Lov } from 'choerodon-ui/pro';

const { DynamicFilterBar } = Table;

<div>
  <DynamicFilterBar
    dataSet={this.ds}
    queryDataSet={this.ds.queryDataSet}
    queryFields={[
      <TextField dataSet={this.ds.queryDataSet} name="name" isFlat/>,
      <NumberField dataSet={this.ds.queryDataSet} name="age" suffix='Suffix' isFlat/>,
      <Select dataSet={this.ds.queryDataSet} name="sex.text" isFlat/>,
      <Lov dataSet={this.ds.queryDataSet} name="code" isFlat/>,
    ]}
  />
  // Custom use in combination with other components
  // ...
</div>

Users can customize the corresponding properties for each filter component in queryFields, allowing more flexible interaction effects for each filter, such as configuring trigger, suffix, and other operations.

Custom Rendering of Query Fields

Scenario description: If users want to customize the rendering details of each query field component, they can do so by configuring queryFields in the Table.

The following three common requirements are used as examples: ① Add a suffix to the name field ② Add an addonAfter to the age field ③ Remove the default suffix of the filter conditions

The effect is shown in the illustration:

The key code is as follows:

<Table
  searchCode='xxx'
  buttons={['add', 'query', 'remove', 'collapseAll', 'reset']}
  dataSet={this.ds}
  queryFields={{
    name: <TextField suffix='字符后缀'/>,// You can perform more custom operations here, such as event handling and removing the default suffix of query fields.
    age: <NumberField addonAfter="%"/>,
    status: <TextField suffix={null}/>
  }}
>

In addition to the above operations, you can also configure more properties of the component at this level, such as event handlers like onClick, custom inline styles in style, and more.

Explanation of the Query Field Effect Without a Label

When the label configuration in the query field information is empty, a placeholder saying "Please enter" will be added by default to optimize the display; if a value is present, the default "Please enter" will disappear.

The effect is as follows:

The key code example is as follows:

const ds = new DataSet({
  ...,
  queryFields: [
    { name: 'empty', type: 'string' },
    ...,
  ]
})

Quick Configuration for Table Display Columns

Scenario Description: Implement custom control over table column display

suffixes/prefixes: Rendering area for filter suffixes/prefixes, where users can customize rendering nodes. This property supports an array type, and if configured as 'filter', it will render as a column display filter to enable custom control over column display.

<Table
  ...
  dataSet={this.ds}
  queryBar="filterBar"
  queryBarProps={{
    dynamicFilterBar: {
      prefixes: ['filter'，<Icon type="assignment-o" />],
    }
  }}
  ...
/>

Click to view more dynamic filter bar API support instructions。
